Jó napot kívánok!/Jó napot! Good day!
Jó estét kívánok!/Jó estét! Good evening!
Jó éjszakat!/Jo éjt! Good night!

rules.ruleText: **Hogy vagytok?** → How are you all? (informal plural)
**-ban** = for **low vowel words** (a, á, o, ó, u, ú)
**-ben** = for **high/mixed vowel words** (i, í, é, e, ö, ő, ü, ű)
**"A könyv a házban van"** = The book is in the house
**"A macska a dobozban van"** = The cat is in the box
**"Autóban ülök"** = I'm sitting in the car
**-n/-on** = for **low vowel words** (a, á, o, ó, u, ú)
**-en/-ön** = for **high/mixed vowel words** (i, í, é, e, ö, ő, ü, ű)
**"A könyv az asztalon van"** = The book is on the table
**"A macska a padon van"** = The cat is on the bench
**"A madár a házon van"** = The bird is on the house
Referring to containers, buildings, enclosed spaces
in New York → **New Yorkban** (some cities use -ban)
Low vowels (a, á, o, ó, u, ú) → -ban, -n/-on
High/mixed vowels (i, í, é, e, ö, ő, ü, ű) → -ben, -en/-ön
**High (Front) vowels**: i, í, é, ö, ő, ü, ű
**Low (Back) vowels**: a, á, o, ó, u, ú
szék → szék**em** (my chair) - ends in consonant, high vowel word
kert → kert**em** (my garden) - ends in consonant, mixed vowel word (takes high endings)
szőke → szők**ém** (my blonde) - ends in vowel "e", high vowel word
ház → ház**am** (my house) - ends in consonant, low vowel word
autó → aut**óm** (my car) - ends in vowel "ó", low vowel word
macska → macsk**ám** (my cat) - ends in vowel "a", low vowel word
szék → szék**ed** (your chair) - ends in consonant, high vowel word
kert → kert**ed** (your garden) - ends in consonant, mixed vowel word
szőke → szők**éd** (your blonde) - ends in vowel "e", high vowel word
ház → ház**ad** (your house) - ends in consonant, low vowel word
autó → aut**ód** (your car) - ends in vowel "ó", low vowel word
macska → macsk**ád** (your cat) - ends in vowel "a", low vowel word
ház → ház**a** (his/her house) - ends in consonant
autó → autó**ja** (his/her car) - ends in vowel "ó"
macska → macsk**ája** (his/her cat) - ends in vowel "a"
kert → kert**e** (his/her garden) - ends in consonant, mixed vowel word (takes high endings)
szék → szék**e** (his/her chair) - ends in consonant, high vowel word
szőke → szők**éje** (his/her blonde) - ends in vowel "e", high vowel word
Low vowel words → use -a/-o/-u linking vowels
High vowel words → use -e/-ö/-ü linking vowels
Mixed vowel words (e) → usually take high vowel endings
**Indefinite conjugation** - when the object is indefinite or there's no object
**Definite conjugation** - when the object is definite (has definite article or is specific)
The object is indefinite (no article or "egy" = a/an)
**-ok** = after low vowel words (a, á, o, ó, u, ú)
**-ek/-ök** = after high vowel words (i, í, é, e, ö, ő, ü, ű)
**-ek** = after unrounded high vowels (i, í, é, e)
**-ol/-el/-öl** = after certain verb stems ending in consonants
The object is definite (has definite article "a/az" or "the")
The object is a proper noun
The object is a possessive form (my, your, etc.)
**"Látok egy házat"** = I see a house (indefinite)
**"Látom a házat"** = I see the house (definite)
**"Magyarul tanulok"** = I learn Hungarian (indefinite - language is indefinite)
**"Tanulom a leckét"** = I learn the lesson (definite)
**"Beszélem a nyelvet"** = I speak the language (definite)
**"Élem az életet"** = I live the life (definite)
**"Szeretem a könyvet"** = I like the book (definite)
**"Veszek egy könyvet"** = I buy a book (indefinite)
**"Veszem a könyvet"** = I buy the book (definite)
**"Iszom a vizet"** = I drink the water (definite)
**"Eszem az almát"** = I eat the apple (definite)
megyitek (you all go it) - rarely used
jövitek (you all come it) - rarely used
Use **indefinite** when object is indefinite or there's no object
Use **definite** when object is definite (has "a/az" or is specific)
Practice with verbs ending in different vowels
Pay attention to consonant changes in some verbs (vesz → veszem)
